failedAssertions.reason: Export JSON on the populated demo report route fails with a Convex ArgumentValidationError for demo-study instead of downloading a file.
Export HTML on the populated demo report route fails with a Convex ArgumentValidationError for demo-study instead of downloading a file.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: The browser surface could not prove the exact three-role JWT model or mutation-level requireRole() enforcement; only researcher-level self-service access was reachable.
No browser-visible path produced a real reviewer session; a self-service reviewer-named signup still exposed Create Study researcher controls.
No browser-visible path produced a real admin session; a self-service admin-named signup still hit admin diagnostics access denied.
Researcher denial at /settings was confirmed, but the admin-render half of the assertion could not be exercised because no browser-visible admin claims path exists.
Settings categories could not be validated because the browser surface never provided an admin-capable session to open /settings.
Domain allowlist management could not be exercised because the browser surface never provided an admin-capable settings session.
The browser surface exposed a forbidden-action launch error, but not the raw validateStudyLaunch { pass, reasons[] } result specified by the assertion.
The production launch confirmation flow auto-acknowledges on confirm; the browser offered no way to submit a production launch without ack and observe rejection.
No real run reached blocked_by_guardrail from the browser surface, so a run detail showing the violated rule could not be exercised.
Credential masking in screenshots/logs requires a live credentialed run and artifact inspection, which were not reachable from browser-only validation.
Whether encrypted credential payloads are stripped from frontend query responses is a backend/data-contract assertion, not directly user-visible in the browser.
Cumulative-failure auto-cancel requires real run execution and remaining queued runs, which were not reachable from browser-only hardening validation.
Admin diagnostics could not be validated because the browser surface never provided an admin-capable session.
Audit-event recording is not directly user-visible without the admin diagnostics surface, which remained inaccessible from the browser.
Audit-trail filtering could not be exercised because the admin diagnostics surface was unreachable from the browser.
Standardized infra error codes are presented on the admin diagnostics page, but that surface was unreachable without an admin role.
Lifecycle metrics are presented on the admin diagnostics page, but the browser surface provided no admin session to load that page.
Signed artifact URL signature and expiry behavior are not directly provable from browser-visible UI alone.
Per-task-category model routing is an admin/backend behavior that is not directly observable from the browser surface.
Budget-cap enforcement depends on admin settings changes and live execution; neither was reachable from the browser surface.
Shared URL auth behavior was validated manually, but direct Copy Link verification was blocked under agent-browser because navigator.clipboard.writeText returned Write permission denied.
guardrailEvents auditing is a backend-table assertion and was not directly observable from the browser surface.
A live pack→study→analysis→report pipeline is still not reachable from the browser surface for a fresh self-service org; the populated demo report exports also fail.

The real browser surface provides no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; self-service password signups named reviewer/admin still landed with researcher-level UI, so role-specific behavior could not be exercised from user testing.
Credential-ref leakage requires a live study configured with credentials plus run artifacts to inspect; that flow was not reachable from the browser surface.
The frontend-to-ArtifactStore boundary is not directly observable from the browser surface without code or network-contract inspection.
Subsequent-study effects require admin settings changes, but the browser surface never exposed an admin-capable settings session.
Concurrent-study isolation requires two live completed studies with separate variants, runs, and analysis outputs; browser-only hardening validation never reached live completions.
Error-recovery integrity requires inducing an analysis failure and then inspecting preserved run/milestone state, which is not directly observable from the browser surface.

The real browser surface provides no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; self-service password signups named reviewer/admin still landed with researcher-level UI, so role-specific behavior could not be exercised from user testing.

appliedUpdates.description: Recorded hardening browser-validation findings: self-service signups never surfaced admin/reviewer roles, demo report exports fail because demo-study is not a real study id, shared report auth still works via /studies/demo-study/report?shared=1, clipboard copy is blocked under agent-browser, and forbidden-action launch errors surface in the study UI.
